Issues.Title: [dnceng-bot] Branch `winforms/release/9.0` can't be merged to Azdo internal branch
Issues.Body:
Branch [release/9.0](https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/commits/release/9.0/) in `dotnet/winforms` repo can't be mirrored from GitHub to Azure [internal/release/9.0](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/internal/_git/dotnet-winforms?version=GBinternal%2Frelease%2F9.0&_a=history) via internal merge. 

There are conflicting commits in the target branch.
The branch is configured to be mirrored via automated merge. 
The situation is rare but normal. The target branch is expected to receive manual commits in the Azure DevOps clone, which could cause conflict.

Conflicts need to be **resolved to unblock the code flow**.

- you can resolve the issue by resolving the conflict in the **target branch only**. 
    - Please make sure that the changes sensitive from security perspective are not published ahead of time to GitHub!
- reverting the extra commits in the Azdo target branch.
- checking the logs of the mirroring process in the [mirroring pipeline](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/internal/_build?definitionId=1387).
- you can disable the mirroring of the branch in the [configuration file](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/internal/_git/dotnet-mirroring?path=/dnceng-subscriptions.jsonc).
- you can ask @dotnet/dnceng for help or read the [documentation](https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/internal/_git/dotnet-mirroring?path=/README.md).

Please close this issue once the conflict is resolved.

Issues.Title: Fix dark mode: CheckBox/RadioButton checked state invisible with Standard FlatStyle, ProgressBar BackColor leaks through
Issues.Body:
Two dark mode regressions fixed: CheckBox/RadioButton with `FlatStyle.Standard + Appearance.Button` renders no visible checked/unchecked distinction in dark mode; ProgressBar unexpectedly reflects user-set `BackColor`/`ForeColor` in dark mode (whereas Visual Styles suppress these in classic mode).

## CheckBox / RadioButton (#14347)

`OwnerDraw` was explicitly `false` for `DarkMode + Standard + Appearance.Button` to avoid `VisualStyleRenderer` HighDPI issues, handing rendering back to the native control — which doesn't differentiate checked state in dark mode.

**Fix:** `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` doesn't use `VisualStyleRenderer` at all, so the workaround is unnecessary. `CreateButtonAdapter()` now returns `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` in dark mode, and the `OwnerDraw` override is simplified to `base.OwnerDraw`.

- `CheckBox.cs` / `RadioButton.cs`: Remove dark-mode special-case from `OwnerDraw`
- `CheckBoxStandardAdapter.cs` / `RadioButtonStandardAdapter.cs`: `CreateButtonAdapter()` returns `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` when `Application.IsDarkModeEnabled`; removed now-redundant `private new ButtonStandardAdapter ButtonAdapter` cast

`ToPushButtonState` already maps `CheckState.Checked → PushButtonState.Pressed`, so the pressed visual correctly reflects the checked state.

## ProgressBar (#11938)

`SetWindowTheme(HWND, " ", " ")` disables Visual Styles in dark mode, making `PBM_SETBKCOLOR`/`PBM_SETBARCOLOR` effective — so user-set colors like `BackColor = Color.Red` bleed through. In classic mode Visual Styles silently suppress these.

**Fix:** Pin dark mode colors to `SystemColors.ControlDarkDark` (background) and `SystemColors.Highlight` (bar) regardless of the `BackColor`/`ForeColor` properties, matching classic-mode semantics where those properties have no visible effect.

- Added `s_darkModeBackColor = SystemColors.ControlDarkDark`
- `OnCreateControl`: after `SetWindowTheme`, explicitly send `PBM_SETBKCOLOR(s_darkModeBackColor)` and `PBM_SETBARCOLOR(s_defaultForeColor)`
- `OnBackColorChanged` / `OnForeColorChanged`: skip `PBM_SET*` when `IsDarkModeEnabled`
- `OnHandleCreated` / `UserPreferenceChangedHandler`: use conditional color selection (`IsDarkModeEnabled ? s_darkModeBackColor : BackColor`)

## Tests

Added `CheckBoxStandardAdapterTests` and `RadioButtonStandardAdapterTests` verifying that `CreateButtonAdapter` returns the correct adapter type per mode and that all paint methods are non-throwing in dark mode.

Issues.Title: Prioritize top open WinForms feature asks from issue backlog
Issues.Body:
This PR addresses the request to identify the most important open feature asks in `dotnet/winforms` and provide a prioritized implementation list.  
It delivers a demand- and readiness-weighted shortlist from the current open issue backlog.

- **Prioritization output**
  - Produced a ranked list of top asks, led by:
    1. Generic Host integration (`UseWindowsFormsLifetime`)
    2. WinForms Application Builder modernization
    3. App-level theming (light/dark/custom)
    4. Taskbar progress API support
    5. Control drawing lock/unlock API
    6. Vectorized drawing/bitmap operations
    7. Expanded `ListViewGroup` capabilities
    8. `DataGridView.Sort` overload for glyph direction

- **Ranking criteria applied**
  - Demand signals: issue discussion volume and community reactions
  - Strategic fit: modernization/performance/accessibility/platform parity
  - Execution readiness: milestone targeting, ownership signals, and triage labels

- **Deliverable characteristics**
  - Focused on open asks (primarily `api-suggestion` / `enhancement`)
  - Optimized for roadmap intake and planning review
  - No product/runtime source changes in this PR; this is backlog-prioritization output

Issues.Title: [Cherry-pick from main]: Skipping Analyzer tests on x86 due to OOM exception.
Issues.Body:
**Issue:** New packages coming in the [PR](https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/pull/14349) are again causing analyzer tests to fail on x86 platform. The recently added ForceGC attribute is not helping with these new changes.

**Fix:**
a. Skipping analyzer tests on x86 platform
b. Added '--ignore-exit-code 8' switch in PR builds to avoid build failure as xunit returns exit code 8 when the assembly has no tests to execute.

Issues.RelatedDescription: Closed or merged PR "Test analyzer oom" (#14336)

Issues.Title: Skipping Analyzer tests on x86 due to OOM exception.
Issues.Body:
**Issue**: New packages coming in the [codeflow PR](https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/pull/14334) are again causing analyzer tests to fail on x86 platform. The recently added ForceGC attribute is not helping with these new changes.

Fix:
1. Skipping analyzer tests on x86 platform 
2. Added '--ignore-exit-code 8' switch in PR builds to avoid build failure as xunit returns exit code 8 when the assembly has no tests to execute.

Issues.Title: [DarkMode] the checked/unchecked items cannot be seen after clicking the CustomComCtl32Button button in DarkMode for WinformsControlTest project
Issues.Body: ### .NET version main branch of Winforms repo ### Did it work in .NET Framework? Not tested/verified ### Did it work in any of the earlier releases of .NET Core or .NET 5+? Repro in Release/10.0 branch, not reproduce Release/9.0 branch ### Issue description The checked/unchecked items cannot be seen after clicking the CustomComCtl32Button button in DarkMode https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76e1c085-f5ea-491c-a49a-84c81bade82e ### Steps to reproduce Test the sample app: [WinformsControlTest ](https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/tree/main/src/System.Windows.Forms/tests/IntegrationTests/DesignSurface/WinformsControlTest ) in Winforms repo, then clicking the CustomComCtl32Button button in DarkMode Or using attached project [ReproduceApplication.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25731489/ReproduceApplication.zip) **More info:** The issue can reproduce when setting the FlatStyle as Standard/System, not reproduce when setting the FaltStyle as Flat/Popup.

Issues.Title: [release/11.0-preview2]: Fix for OOM exception while running Analyzer tests on x86 platform
Issues.Body:
Cherry pick from PR:https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/pull/14328

**Issue**: Analyzer tests are running into OutOfMemory exceptions on x86 platform. This is because multiple Roslyn compilations are held into memory which causes memory usage to shoot up before GC gets a chance to collect the finalized objects. On x86 the memory space is 2 GB which runs into OOM.

This issue doesn't repro on x64 or when these tests are executed locally in isolation.

**Fix**: To run GC.Collect after every test execution
